patch.value: Lazy extension activation: defer import+activate until a command needs contributions

Extension overhead is ~73ms on EVERY command in a repo with extensions installed (measured: dev repo list 240ms vs 165ms with --no-extensions; 8 bundled extensions). loader.ts loadExtensions (line ~689) does await import(importHref) for each discovered extension and activateExtensions runs every activate() on every command, even for core commands (list/get) that may need no extension contributions. Investigate deferring the expensive import()+activate() until a command actually requires an extension's command/renderer/parser/hook/service — using cheap manifest capability metadata to decide. Must preserve renderer/hook overrides for core commands. Distinct from pm-3mal (which is module-load/completion/MCP-schema/telemetry-flush, not extension activation). Scales with number of installed extensions.
Commands that use no extension contributions skip extension import()/activate(); renderer/hook overrides still apply where needed; measurable per-command savings with N extensions
